title: Create canonical VERSION file and versioning contract specification
summary: Create the single source-of-truth VERSION file at the repository root containing the initial semantic version (0.1.0). Include docs/VERSIONING.md defining the semantic versioning contract: how VERSION relates to CFBundleShortVersionString in Info.plist, AGENT_VERSION in Python's version.py, version in pyproject.toml, and the version handshake protocol between Swift shell and Python backend. Define version bump rules (major = breaking IPC/contract change, minor = new capability, patch = bugfix), pre-release tag format, and the CI enforcement rule that all representations must match at build time.

title: Establish cross-TRD conflict resolution hierarchy and ambiguity resolutions
summary: Create docs/CONFLICT_RESOLUTION.md defining the complete TRD precedence hierarchy: TRD-11 (security) overrides all TRDs on security matters → owning TRD governs its own domain → standards docs (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md) break ties → best-practice defaults. Document all specific ambiguity resolutions across the 16 TRDs: startup timeout values (TRD-1 vs TRD-12), overlapping code quality gates (TRD-3 vs TRD-14), configuration key naming conflicts, error taxonomy overlaps, and logging level definitions. Each resolution includes the conflicting sources, the chosen resolution, and the rationale. Also create forge-standards/decision-precedence.md as a quick-reference lookup.

title: Define product identity, naming conventions, and branding rules
summary: Create docs/PRODUCT_IDENTITY.md defining: 'Forge' as user-facing product name, 'Dev Agent' as subheader, usage rules for code vs UI vs docs, and prohibited forms. Create docs/NAMING_CONVENTIONS.md with comprehensive rules: branch naming (feature/<prd>-<pr>-<slug>, fix/<issue>-<slug>, release/<semver>), file naming (snake_case Python, PascalCase Swift types, camelCase Swift variables), directory naming (lowercase-hyphenated for docs/config, snake_case Python packages, PascalCase Swift groups), artifact naming (<forge>-<component>-<version>-<arch>.<ext>), state/enum naming (UPPER_SNAKE constants, PascalCase enums), configuration key naming (dot-separated hierarchical: forge.backend.consensus.timeout), and repository layout specification.

title: Bootstrap complete repository directory skeleton
summary: Create the canonical directory structure for the entire project. Swift shell: ForgeApp/ with Sources/ForgeApp/ (App/, IPC/, UI/, Services/), ForgeAgent/, ForgeAgentTests/, Resources/. Python backend: src/forge/ package (consensus/, pipeline/, agents/, github_integration/, review/, document_store/, recovery/, config/, utils/, errors/, contracts/), each with __init__.py. Shared: config/defaults/, docs/ (trds/, prds/, runbooks/, architecture/), forge-standards/, scripts/ (ci/, dev/, setup/), tests/ (unit/, integration/, e2e/, fixtures/), .github/workflows/, contracts/, state/, artifacts/. Include .gitkeep in empty directories. Create .gitignore with Python, Swift, macOS, IDE patterns. Create scripts/validate_structure.py that asserts the directory tree matches the canonical layout for CI enforcement.

title: Tests for repository skeleton and naming convention compliance
summary: Automated tests validating the bootstrapped repository layout and naming conventions from PRs 1-4. Test directory completeness: verify every required directory exists, every __init__.py is present, no unexpected top-level directories. Test naming conventions: scan all Python files for snake_case module names, verify directory naming patterns. Test validate_structure.py itself: run it and verify it passes on the current repo, test that it detects a missing directory. Test presence of canonical documents (VERSION, VERSIONING.md, CONFLICT_RESOLUTION.md, NAMING_CONVENTIONS.md, PRODUCT_IDENTITY.md).

title: Create Python project scaffolding with pyproject.toml and version module
summary: Create pyproject.toml with project metadata (name='forge-dev-agent'), build system configuration (hatchling), dependency groups (core, dev, test, lint), Python version constraint (>=3.11), and version sourced dynamically from the VERSION file. Create src/forge/version.py that reads VERSION file and exposes AGENT_VERSION constant, version_tuple(), and is_compatible(remote_version) for the handshake protocol. Create src/forge/py.typed marker. Create requirements/ directory with base.txt, dev.txt, test.txt for pip-based workflows. Ensure version in pyproject.toml matches VERSION file via a check in version.py module load.

title: Create Swift project scaffolding with Package.swift and version binding
summary: Create the Swift package/Xcode project skeleton: ForgeApp/Package.swift defining the ForgeApp target and ForgeAgentTests test target. Create ForgeApp/Sources/ForgeApp/App/ForgeApp.swift as a minimal SwiftUI app entry point displaying 'Forge' with 'Dev Agent' subheader. Create ForgeApp/Sources/ForgeApp/App/ForgeVersion.swift that reads the root VERSION file at build time and exposes it as a static property. Create ForgeApp/Sources/ForgeApp/Info.plist with CFBundleShortVersionString placeholder. Create ForgeApp/Config/Base.xcconfig, Debug.xcconfig, Release.xcconfig, Version.xcconfig. Create scripts/sync_version.sh that validates VERSION == Info.plist == pyproject.toml == ForgeVersion.swift constant.

title: Define shared error taxonomy and error types
summary: Create the unified error taxonomy used across both Swift and Python. Python: src/forge/errors/base.py defining the hierarchy: ForgeError (base) with subtypes ForgeConfigError, ForgeIPCError, ForgeConsensusError, ForgePipelineError, ForgeGitHubError, ForgeReviewError, ForgeSecurityError, ForgeRecoveryError, ForgeValidationError. Each includes error_code (string, e.g. 'FORGE-IPC-001'), severity (enum: critical, error, warning), is_retryable (bool), and context dict, plus serialization to dict for IPC transport. Swift: ForgeApp/Sources/ForgeApp/Services/ForgeError.swift with matching error enum. Create docs/architecture/error_taxonomy.md documenting all error codes, meanings, retry policies, and TRD ownership per error domain. Resolve overlapping definitions across TRD-1, TRD-12, TRD-13 per CONFLICT_RESOLUTION.md.

title: Define shared configuration model, schema, and defaults
summary: Create the unified configuration system. Python: src/forge/config/settings.py with a Pydantic BaseSettings model (ForgeSettings) defining all configuration keys using dot-separated hierarchical naming with typed defaults, environment variable overrides (FORGE_ prefix), and YAML file loading. src/forge/config/loader.py implementing config precedence: environment vars > user config > project config > defaults. Create config/defaults/forge.yaml with all default values. Create config/forge-config.schema.json as machine-readable JSON Schema. Create src/forge/config/capabilities.py for capability flags (consensus_enabled, review_3pass_enabled, ci_validation_enabled). Swift: ForgeApp/Sources/ForgeApp/Services/ForgeSettings.swift matching structure reading from UserDefaults and shared config. Create docs/architecture/configuration-model.md documenting the full model.

title: Tests for version module, configuration loader, and error types
summary: Comprehensive tests for foundational Python modules from PRs 6, 8, 9. Version tests: AGENT_VERSION matches VERSION file, version_tuple() returns correct tuple, is_compatible() handles major/minor/patch rules. Config tests: ForgeSettings loads defaults from forge.yaml, env overrides with FORGE_ prefix work, config precedence (env > file > defaults), invalid config raises ForgeConfigError, capability flags default correctly, JSON Schema validates defaults. Error tests: hierarchy verification (all errors inherit ForgeError), error_code format validation, severity enum values, is_retryable defaults per type, serialization to dict for IPC, context dict round-trip.

title: Define IPC interface contracts and message schemas
summary: Create machine-readable interface contracts for Swift-Python IPC. Create docs/architecture/ipc_contract.md documenting: Unix domain socket path, JSON message envelope ({"version", "msg_type", "correlation_id", "payload"}), all message types (ready_handshake, build_intent, pipeline_status, review_result, approval_request, approval_response, error), version compatibility rules. Python: src/forge/contracts/message_types.py (enum), schemas.py (Pydantic models per envelope/payload), handshake.py (version compatibility logic). Swift: ForgeApp/Sources/ForgeApp/IPC/IPCMessageTypes.swift with matching Codable structs. Machine-readable: contracts/forge-ipc-api.json and docs/architecture/ipc_schema.json for cross-language validation. Create contracts/ready-handshake.json specifying startup handshake sequence and compatibility negotiation.

title: Tests for IPC contracts, message schemas, and handshake logic
summary: Comprehensive tests for IPC contracts from PR-11. Message types: all expected types defined, enum values unique, string representations match contract doc. Schemas: each Pydantic model validates correct payloads, rejects malformed payloads, serializes to JSON matching ipc_schema.json, round-trips through JSON. Handshake: version compatibility logic (same major = compatible, different major = incompatible, newer minor backend = compatible with warning), ready-handshake sequence validation. Schema validation: load ipc_schema.json and validate all example messages. Cross-language consistency: Python message type strings match Swift IPCMessageTypes values from documentation.

title: Create AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and architecture context documents
summary: Create AI agent context files guiding all subsequent development. AGENTS.md: project context including directory layout reference, version contract reference, naming convention reference, TRD precedence hierarchy reference, coding standards (Python: black, ruff, mypy strict; Swift: SwiftLint, SwiftFormat), commit message format (conventional commits), PR template, list of all 16 TRDs with summaries. CLAUDE.md: AI-specific instructions including conflict resolution hierarchy, security-first principles, TRD-11 override rule, coding constraints. docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md: two-process architecture overview (ASCII diagram), component interaction map, data flow. docs/architecture/system-context.md: broader system context. docs/architecture/source-of-truth-index.md mapping each concern to its canonical file.

title: Create foundational CI workflow for structure, version, and lint validation
summary: Create .github/workflows/foundation-checks.yml running on every PR and push to main. Jobs: (1) validate-structure: runs scripts/validate_structure.py, (2) validate-version-sync: runs scripts/sync_version.sh ensuring VERSION == pyproject.toml == Info.plist == ForgeVersion.swift, (3) validate-contracts: runs scripts/validate_contracts.py checking JSON schemas are valid and contract files present, (4) python-lint: runs ruff check and mypy on src/forge/, (5) python-test: runs pytest on tests/unit/ with coverage, (6) swift-build: builds ForgeApp to verify compilation. Create .github/pull_request_template.md with checklist. Create .pre-commit-config.yaml with hooks for ruff, black, trailing whitespace, version sync. Create scripts/validate_contracts.py and scripts/check_version_sync.py.

title: Create README.md, developer setup, contributing guide, and bootstrap script
summary: Create root README.md with Forge branding, one-paragraph description, architecture overview (two-process: Swift shell + Python backend, dual-LLM consensus, 8-stage pipeline), prerequisites (macOS 14+, Xcode 15+, Python 3.11+), quick start guide, links to architecture docs, VERSIONING.md, contributing guidelines. Create scripts/setup/bootstrap.sh: creates Python venv, installs requirements, runs validate_structure.py, runs sync_version.sh, installs pre-commit hooks, validates contracts. Create CONTRIBUTING.md with branch naming rules, PR process, commit message format, references to NAMING_CONVENTIONS.md. Create LICENSE placeholder. Create docs/development/bootstrap-workflow.md documenting the full onboarding flow.

title: Document PRD-001 baseline decisions and publish source-of-truth index
summary: Create docs/prds/PRD-001.md as the finalized record of all foundational decisions made across the preceding PRs: versioning contract, conflict resolution outcomes, naming conventions adopted, directory layout rationale, IPC contract baseline, error taxonomy, configuration model, and capability flags. Cross-reference every foundational concern to its canonical file via docs/architecture/source-of-truth-index.md (updating it from PR-13 with final file paths). This serves as the onboarding index for all subsequent PRDs and provides traceability from requirements to implementation artifacts.

title: Integration tests for full bootstrap and cross-component consistency
summary: End-to-end integration tests validating the entire foundation works together. Bootstrap test: run scripts/setup/bootstrap.sh in clean env, verify venv created, deps install, validate_structure.py passes, sync_version.sh passes, validate_contracts.py passes. Version round-trip: import forge.version, verify AGENT_VERSION matches VERSION, create mock IPC handshake via schemas.py and verify version field. Config-error integration: create ForgeSettings with invalid config, verify ForgeConfigError with correct error_code format. Contract alignment: verify Python message types match contract JSON definitions, handshake schema matches ready-handshake.json. Naming convention scan: verify all Python module names are snake_case, class names PascalCase. Directory completeness: verify every __init__.py and .gitkeep exists.
